task: I’m dealing with a check that may come back into question after the table has already left, and I want to be prepared before anyone asks about it. What kinds of records or details are worth having on hand in that situation?

turns.agent_response:
Have these ready:

- Table number
- Exact time the check was closed
- Closed check copy
- Signed receipt, if there is one
- Original ticket
- Total amount
- Last 4 digits of the card used
- What the guest said was wrong
- Who you handed the issue to afterward

If you want, I can turn that into a quick shift note format you can reuse.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s the right stuff. Missing the part that matters if they start trying to wiggle out of it later:

- any handwritten notes from the server or manager
- itemized modifiers / comps / voids
- whether the card was authorized and how it was run
- names of the people who touched the check after it got questioned
- timestamped POS logs if the system keeps them

And if there was any argument at the table, write down the exact wording while it’s still fresh. People change their story fast once they’re gone.

If you want, I’d rather have a tight shift-note version than the generic list.

turns.agent_response:
Yes — for a check that may get disputed after the table leaves, the strongest record is a clean paper trail plus a short factual note.

Worth having on hand:

- Table number
- Exact time the check was opened and closed
- Closed check copy
- Itemized ticket
- Signed receipt, if any
- Total amount
- Last 4 digits of the card used
- Authorization details, if your system shows them
- Any comps, voids, or modifiers tied to the check
- Handwritten notes from server or manager
- Names of anyone who handled the check after it was questioned
- POS timestamps or audit logs, if available
- Exact words the guest used about the problem, if there was a complaint
- What action was taken and by whom

Best practice is to write it down while it’s fresh and keep it factual, not interpretive.

If you want, I can turn that into a tight shift-note template like:

**Table / Time / Issue / Check details / Actions taken / Who handled it next**

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s the thing. Clean paper trail, no extra drama.

That checklist is basically what I’d want:

- table number
- open/close time
- closed check copy
- itemized ticket
- signed receipt if there is one
- total
- last 4 of the card
- auth details if the POS shows them
- comps / voids / modifiers
- handwritten notes
- who touched it after the complaint
- POS timestamps or audit logs
- exact words the guest used
- what was done and by who

And yeah, keep it factual. Not ‘seemed mad’ or whatever. Just what happened. If they start changing the story later, that’s what saves you.

The tight shift-note version is the one I’d actually use.
